subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity of an oil and petrol interceptor must be authorised by the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An oil and petrol interceptor must contain a permanently fixed, ridged baffle constructed of a minimum—

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: PVC-U must not be used for the construction, connection or venting of an oil and petrol interceptor.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An oil and petrol interceptor must be provided with airtight covers that are suitable size and be readily removable by one person.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The inlet and outlet of the oil and petrol interceptor pit is to be covered with either a 600 mm x 600 mm or 600 mm diameter cover.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Ventilation of oil and petrol interceptors must—

ol.li: be extended to open air; and

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An oil and petrol interceptor must be located in a position that enables ready access for maintenance and inspection.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Outlet fittings from an oil and petrol interceptor must be manufactured from one piece of DN 100 cast iron pipe.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An oil and petrol interceptor must be protected from traffic loads by a suitable cover.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: When an oil and petrol interceptor is connected to the Network Utility Operator's sewerage system. it must comply with the requirements of the Network Utility Operator's guidelines for pre-treatment devices.

callout.p: Where the Permit Authority has not specified the capacity of an oil and petrol interceptor in accordance with C4D16(1), it must be no less than 11400 litres, below the outlet.
See TAS Figure C4D16 for a typical oil and petrol interceptor appliance.
